statement.focus_question: Call $n$ weird if $\sigma(n)\geq 2n$ and $n$ is not pseudoperfect, that is, it is not the sum of any set of its divisors. Are there any odd weird numbers?
statement.normalized: Call $n$ weird if $\sigma(n)\geq 2n$ and $n$ is not pseudoperfect, that is, it is not the sum of any set of its divisors. Are there any odd weird numbers? Are there infinitely many primitive weird numbers, i.e. those such that no proper divisor of $n$ is weird?
statement.original:
Call $n$ weird if $\sigma(n)\geq 2n$ and $n$ is not pseudoperfect, that is, it is not the sum of any set of its divisors.

Are there any odd weird numbers? Are there infinitely many primitive weird numbers, i.e. those such that no proper divisor of $n$ is weird?
